days.briefing: Global aviation security remains under significant pressure on multiple fronts, with GPS spoofing attacks confirmed at major Indian airports, cyber intrusions targeting airline booking and catering supply chains across Asia-Pacific and Europe, and escalating cartel drone incursions forcing temporary closures of U.S. civil airspace. Regulators in Europe (EASA Part-IS) and internationally (ICAO Second Edition Global Aviation Security Plan) are tightening binding cybersecurity and UAS threat frameworks, but compliance gaps and fragmented counter-drone architectures continue to leave critical vulnerabilities.

items.title: GPS Spoofing & Jamming Confirmed at Eight Indian Airports
items.summary: India's Civil Aviation Minister confirmed GPS spoofing and jamming at Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Bangalore, Chennai, and Amritsar airports, forcing pilots onto backup navigation procedures.

items.title: Cartel Drone Incursion Forces El Paso Airport Airspace Closure
items.summary: Alleged cartel drones breached US airspace near El Paso International Airport; DoD neutralised threat, FAA closed airspace—only second such closure since 9/11.

items.title: Aviation Booking Software Provider Hit by MFA-Fatigue Phishing Campaign
items.summary: A booking software provider serving multiple major airlines suffered credential compromise via MFA fatigue, granting attackers access to Microsoft 365, cloud admin, and OT systems.

items.title: Korean Air Discloses 30,000 Employee Records Stolen via Oracle EBS Supply-Chain Hack
items.summary: Korean Air confirmed hackers stole names and bank account details of ~30,000 employees via a breach of catering supplier KC&D linked to the Oracle EBS zero-day campaign.

items.title: EASA Part-IS Information Security Regulation Now Fully Applicable Across EU Aviation Ecosystem
items.summary: EASA Part-IS binding cybersecurity rules, covering airlines, MROs, airports and ANSPs, reached full applicability on 22 February 2026, requiring structured ISMS aligned with aviation safety objectives.

items.title: ICAO Assembly Adopts Second Edition Global Aviation Security Plan with UAS and GNSS Threat Mandate
items.summary: ICAO's 192-member Assembly endorsed the Second Edition Global Aviation Security Plan and directed a dedicated work programme on weaponised UAS and GNSS radio frequency interference in conflict zones.
